indicators.title: Unemployment rate
indicators.notes: Headline CPS unemployment rate, U-3.

indicators.title: U-6 labor underutilization rate
indicators.notes: Broad labor underutilization measure including unemployed, marginally attached, and part-time for economic reasons. BLS is canonical; FRED U6RATE is only a convenience mirror.

indicators.title: U-1 unemployment rate, 15 weeks or longer
indicators.notes: Alternative labor underutilization measure U-1; people unemployed 15 weeks or longer as a percent of the civilian labor force.

indicators.title: U-2 unemployment rate, job losers
indicators.notes: Alternative labor underutilization measure U-2; job losers and people who completed temporary jobs as a percent of the civilian labor force. BLS series `LNS14023621` is the verified seasonally adjusted rate; `LNS13025671` is not a valid BLS series.

indicators.title: U-4 labor underutilization rate
indicators.notes: Alternative labor underutilization measure U-4; total unemployed plus discouraged workers as a percent of the civilian labor force plus discouraged workers.

indicators.title: U-5 labor underutilization rate
indicators.notes: Alternative labor underutilization measure U-5; total unemployed plus all marginally attached workers as a percent of the civilian labor force plus all marginally attached workers.

indicators.title: Average hourly earnings of all employees, total private
Average weekly hours of all employees, total private
Average weekly earnings of all employees, total private
indicators.notes: BLS CES total-private all-employees weekly earnings sibling to average hourly earnings and average weekly hours; BLS API verified on 2026-06-03.

indicators.title: Hires rate, total nonfarm
indicators.notes: BLS JOLTS API returned observations for this all-nonfarm seasonally adjusted series on 2026-06-01; included as a flow companion to hires level.

indicators.title: Total separations level, total nonfarm
indicators.notes: BLS JOLTS API returned observations for this all-nonfarm seasonally adjusted series on 2026-06-01; total separations are the broad outflow from jobs.

indicators.title: Total separations rate, total nonfarm
indicators.notes: BLS JOLTS API returned observations for this all-nonfarm seasonally adjusted series on 2026-06-01; total separations rate complements quits and layoffs rates.

indicators.title: Quits level, total nonfarm
indicators.notes: BLS JOLTS API returned observations for this all-nonfarm seasonally adjusted series on 2026-06-01; quits are voluntary job separations.

indicators.title: Layoffs and discharges level, total nonfarm
indicators.notes: BLS JOLTS API returned observations for this all-nonfarm seasonally adjusted series on 2026-06-01; layoffs and discharges are involuntary job separations.

indicators.title: Layoffs and discharges rate, total nonfarm
Other separations level, total nonfarm
indicators.notes: Completes the JOLTS separations decomposition with quits and layoffs/discharges; BLS API verified on 2026-06-03.

indicators.title: Other separations rate, total nonfarm
indicators.notes: Completes the JOLTS separations rate decomposition with quits and layoffs/discharges; BLS API verified on 2026-06-03.

indicators.title: Initial unemployment insurance claims, seasonally adjusted
indicators.notes: FRED mirror for hosted-dashboard claims display while DOL API access is rate-limited; use `initial_claims_sa_dol` for DOL-origin redistributable/offline export once DOL fetches reliably.

indicators.title: Continued unemployment insurance claims, seasonally adjusted
indicators.notes: FRED mirror for hosted-dashboard claims display while DOL API access is rate-limited; use `continued_claims_sa_dol` for DOL-origin redistributable/offline export once DOL fetches reliably.

indicators.title: Initial unemployment insurance claims, seasonally adjusted (DOL origin)
indicators.notes: DOL/ETA national weekly claims endpoint and `c5` seasonally adjusted initial claims field confirmed; live refresh is currently DOL-rate/server limited, so hosted dashboards use the FRED mirror `initial_claims_sa` until DOL fetches reliably.

indicators.title: Continued unemployment insurance claims, seasonally adjusted (DOL origin)
indicators.notes: DOL/ETA national weekly claims endpoint and `c7` seasonally adjusted continued claims field confirmed; live refresh is currently DOL-rate/server limited, so hosted dashboards use the FRED mirror `continued_claims_sa` until DOL fetches reliably.

indicators.title: Other marginally attached workers
indicators.notes: BLS CPS reasons-other-than-discouragement marginally attached series; only the not-seasonally-adjusted `LNU05026648` series validated on 2026-06-03, while `LNS15026648` does not exist.

indicators.title: Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers, all items

indicators.notes: FRED mirror ID is declared; verify BEA-origin endpoint before any redistributable public export.

indicators.title: Employment Cost Index, wages and salaries, private industry workers

sources.title: BLS Public Data API
sources.access: API v2 JSON/XLSX; optional registration key for higher limits and more historical access.
sources.notes: Use BLS as canonical source for labor series when API IDs are known. For large historical pulls, prefer BLS flat files or FRED mirrors for convenience.
preferred_for: CES payroll employment, hours, and earnings
JOLTS openings, hires, quits, layoffs, separations
Business Employment Dynamics gross job gains/losses
LAUS state and local labor force data

sources.title: BLS QCEW Open Data CSV slices
sources.access: Keyless CSV endpoints sliced by year/quarter and area, industry, or establishment size.
sources.notes: Large files. Keep raw downloads out of git and export only compact chart-ready aggregations.

sources.title: FRED API
sources.owner: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis
sources.notes: Convenient for source-agnostic series and fast dashboard prototyping. Preserve original source attribution in labels.
preferred_for: BEA/BLS series where FRED mirror is sufficient

sources.title: Census QWI / LEHD API
sources.access: Census API. API key currently required by QWI documentation.
sources.notes: National data is not yet available through the QWI API documentation; state/county/metro are primary.
preferred_for: hires, separations, earnings, and employment by worker demographics
firm age and firm size detail

sources.title: DOL/ETA Unemployment Insurance Weekly Claims
sources.owner: U.S. Department of Labor, Employment and Training Administration
sources.access: DOL Open Data Portal API v4.
sources.notes: National endpoint confirmed as ETA/ui_national_weekly_claims. Use rptdate for week ending date, c5 for seasonally adjusted initial claims, and c7 for seasonally adjusted continued claims. FRED ICSA/CCSA remain fallback mirrors only.
